summary: Comprehensive testing of FinFlow enhancement features completed. Backend: 11/11 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All new UI features verified working. Critical bug fixed: theme.ts renamed to theme.tsx to resolve JSX syntax error. All enhancement features functional.

issues: Console deprecation warnings: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' and 'shadow* style props are deprecated' - React Native Web compatibility warnings, not critical (same as iteration 1)

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: theme.ts contained JSX but had .ts extension - renamed to theme.tsx to resolve Metro bundler syntax error
All new APIs properly implemented with correct status codes and error handling
MongoDB _id properly excluded from all responses
All new UI components have proper testID attributes
Recurring expenses logic correctly implemented with monthly processing
Due day field properly validated (1-31 range)
Settings modal properly implemented as modal presentation
History and Month Detail screens properly implemented with navigation
Export PDF functionality implemented using expo-print and expo-sharing
Theme persistence using AsyncStorage working correctly
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created. Used existing data from iteration 1. Created test data during testing: 1 bill with due day (Test Electric, $150, due day 5), 1 recurring expense (Netflix Subscription, $15.99, recurring=true)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All enhancement features tested and working. Backend APIs for monthly history, monthly detail, recurring expenses, toggle recurring, and reset all functional. Frontend onboarding flow (3 steps), settings modal (theme/currency/reset), history screen, month detail with export, bills with due day field and 'due soon' badge, expenses with recurring toggle and badge all verified working. Critical fix applied: theme.ts renamed to theme.tsx. No bugs found in enhancement features.

critical_fix.issue: Metro bundler SyntaxError: theme.ts contained JSX but had .ts extension
critical_fix.root_cause: TypeScript files with JSX must use .tsx extension, not .ts
critical_fix.verification: Cleared Metro cache, restarted Expo, app bundled successfully

rca_of_issue: The main agent created theme.ts with JSX content (ThemeProvider component with JSX return statement). TypeScript requires .tsx extension for files containing JSX syntax. Metro bundler failed to parse JSX in .ts file, causing syntax error. Fix: renamed to .tsx, cleared caches, restarted Expo. All features now working correctly.
